skill.name: skill-i18n
skill.description: Translate SKILL.md and README.md files into localized skill documentation for international users.
skill.summary: Generate localized skill documentation while preserving code blocks, paths, commands, and identifiers.
skill.author: guo-yu

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review shows most are false positives from Markdown examples, language codes, and localized text. The skill still has real medium-risk behavior because it instructs agents to run shell snippets and read or write Claude configuration files under the user home directory. No malicious intent, network exfiltration, credential harvesting, or prompt injection override was found.

medium_findings.title: Legitimate Home Directory Configuration Access
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to read and update a Claude configuration file under the user home directory. This matches the skill purpose, but it can modify persistent user configuration and should be disclosed.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced home-directory configuration file and read/write steps are explicit in the skill instructions. The behavior is not malicious, but it is a real filesystem side effect.

medium_findings.title: Shell Command Workflow Instructions
medium_findings.description: The skill includes shell snippets for checking files, reading configuration, using jq, and moving a temporary file into place. These commands appear bounded to skill documentation workflows, but agents may execute them during use.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible in execution-step sections and are likely meant to guide agent behavior. No network calls, credential harvesting, or arbitrary user-controlled command construction were found.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Static cryptography alerts appear to match Markdown file extensions, language codes, and documentation text rather than cryptographic code. No hashing, encryption, or password handling implementation was found in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are documentation and localized labels, not executable cryptography code. The evaluation file also records the same false-positive interpretation.

low_findings.title: Localized Markdown Entropy Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: High entropy alerts on translated Markdown files are consistent with Japanese and Chinese UTF-8 text. No encoded payload, binary blob, or hidden executable content was found in those files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The high-entropy files are readable localized Markdown. The signal is expected for multilingual content and does not indicate obfuscation.

low_findings.title: Prior Audit Metadata Is Not Runtime Behavior
low_findings.description: The evaluation file contains prior audit and marketplace metadata, including risk labels and references to configuration paths. It should not be trusted as authority, but it does not instruct the agent to skip review or change behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file includes self-referential audit data, which can bias review if followed. I found no direct prompt injection wording such as an override, system update, or instruction to bypass security analysis.

dangerous_patterns.title: Legitimate Home Directory Configuration Access
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill instructs agents to read and update a Claude configuration file under the user home directory. This matches the skill purpose, but it can modify persistent user configuration and should be disclosed.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The referenced home-directory configuration file and read/write steps are explicit in the skill instructions. The behavior is not malicious, but it is a real filesystem side effect.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Command Workflow Instructions
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes shell snippets for checking files, reading configuration, using jq, and moving a temporary file into place. These commands appear bounded to skill documentation workflows, but agents may execute them during use.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible in execution-step sections and are likely meant to guide agent behavior. No network calls, credential harvesting, or arbitrary user-controlled command construction were found.

content.value_statement: Skill documentation often reaches users who prefer different languages. This skill guides agents to create localized SKILL.md and README.md files while preserving technical content.
actual_capabilities: Translates SKILL.md and README.md content into configured languages.
Generates language-specific files such as SKILL.zh-CN.md and SKILL.ja.md.
Preserves code blocks, file paths, command names, URLs, and identifiers.
Stores default and per-skill language preferences in a Claude config file.
Supports non-interactive flags for integration with other documentation workflows.
limitations: It provides agent instructions, not a standalone translation program.
It can read and write files in skill directories and Claude configuration paths.
Translation quality depends on the model and user review.
Existing translated files may need manual conflict review before overwrite.

use_cases.title: Publish localized skills
use_cases.description: Create translated skill documentation so international users can understand setup, usage, and limitations.

use_cases.title: Update multilingual docs
use_cases.description: Refresh translated SKILL.md or README.md files when the source documentation changes.

use_cases.title: Automate documentation release steps
use_cases.description: Use flags and saved configuration to include translation in a larger skill publishing workflow.

prompt_templates.title: Translate Current Skill
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request in a skill folder
prompt_templates.prompt: Use skill-i18n to translate the current SKILL.md file into the configured default languages.

prompt_templates.title: Choose Languages
prompt_templates.prompt: Use skill-i18n to translate SKILL.md into zh-CN and ja. Preserve code blocks, commands, file paths, and URLs.

prompt_templates.title: Translate Multiple Files
prompt_templates.scenario: Documentation includes both skill and repository docs
prompt_templates.prompt: Use skill-i18n to translate SKILL.md and README.md for this skill. Ask before overwriting existing translations.

prompt_templates.title: Run Non-Interactive Localization
prompt_templates.prompt: Use skill-i18n with explicit language and file settings for a named skill, and avoid interactive prompts unless required.

output_examples.input: Translate the current skill into Simplified Chinese and Japanese.
output: Reports that the source file was translated.
Lists the generated localized files by language.
Confirms whether configuration was saved for future runs.

output_examples.input: Translate SKILL.md and README.md for a named skill without prompts.
output: Summarizes each source file that was processed.
Notes skipped or existing files that need user review.

output_examples.input: Configure default languages for future skill translations.
output_examples.output: Returns a short confirmation with selected languages, selected files, and the affected skill configuration.

best_practices: Review translated documentation before publishing it to users.
Confirm overwrite behavior before replacing existing localized files.
Keep code blocks, paths, commands, and identifiers unchanged during translation.
anti_patterns: Using it on repositories where translated files should not be written.
Assuming technical examples were translated when they are preserved by design.
Running automated translation without checking source documentation quality first.

faq.question: Which files can it translate?
faq.answer: It is designed for SKILL.md and README.md files in skill documentation workflows.

faq.question: Which languages are described?
faq.answer: The skill describes zh-CN, ja, ko, es, and custom language codes.

faq.question: Does it translate code blocks?
faq.answer: No. It preserves code blocks, file paths, commands, URLs, and technical identifiers.

faq.question: Where does it store settings?
faq.answer: It stores defaults and per-skill settings in a Claude configuration file under the user home directory.

faq.question: Can it run without prompts?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented flags support explicit languages, files, skill name, and non-interactive mode.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: It is publishable with a warning because it can execute shell steps and write documentation or configuration files.
